Establishing National Goals To Prevent Teenage Pregnancies, , , provided that:Pub. L. 104–193, title IX, § 905110 Stat. 2349Aug. 22, 1996“(a).—In GeneralNot later than , the Secretary of Health and Human Services shall establish and implement a strategy for—January 1, 1997“(1)preventing out-of-wedlock teenage pregnancies, and“(2)assuring that at least 25 percent of the communities in the United States have teenage pregnancy prevention programs in place.“(b).—ReportNot later than , and annually thereafter, the Secretary shall report to the Congress with respect to the progress that has been made in meeting the goals described in paragraphs (1) and (2) of subsection (a).”June 30, 1998

children.label: A
children.text: the amount appropriated pursuant to subsection (f)(1) for the fiscal year or period, minus the amount reserved under subsection (f)(2) for the fiscal year or period; and

children.label: B
children.text: the proportion that the number of low-income children in the State bears to the total of such numbers of children for all the States.

children.heading: Other entities
children.label: A Other entities
children.text: For the purpose described in subsection (b), the Secretary shall, for each of fiscal years 2018 through 2026 and with respect to fiscal year 2027, for the applicable period described in paragraph (1), for any State which has not transmitted an application for the fiscal year (or, with respect to the applicable period, for fiscal year 2027) under , allot to one or more entities in the State the amount that would have been allotted to the State under paragraph (1) if the State had submitted such an application.section 705(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: not later than 30 days after the deadline for the State involved to submit an application for the fiscal year (or, with respect to fiscal year 2027, for the applicable period described in paragraph (1)) under , the Secretary publishes a notice soliciting grant applications; andsection 705(a) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: not later than 120 days after such deadline, all such applications must be submitted.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except for research under paragraph (5) and information collection and reporting under paragraph (6), the purpose of an allotment under subsection (a) to a State (or to another entity in the State pursuant to subsection (a)(2)) is to enable the State or other entity to implement education exclusively on sexual risk avoidance (meaning voluntarily refraining from sexual activity).

children.label: A
children.text: ensure that the unambiguous and primary emphasis and context for each topic described in paragraph (3) is a message to youth that normalizes the optimal health behavior of avoiding nonmarital sexual activity;

children.label: B
children.text: be medically accurate and complete;

children.label: C
children.text: be age-appropriate;

children.label: D
children.text: be based on adolescent learning and developmental theories for the age group receiving the education; and

children.label: E
children.text: be culturally appropriate, recognizing the experiences of youth from diverse communities, backgrounds, and experiences.

children.label: A
children.text: The holistic individual and societal benefits associated with personal responsibility, self-regulation, goal setting, healthy decisionmaking, and a focus on the future.

children.label: B
children.text: The advantage of refraining from nonmarital sexual activity in order to improve the future prospects and physical and emotional health of youth.

children.label: C
children.text: The increased likelihood of avoiding poverty when youth attain self-sufficiency and emotional maturity before engaging in sexual activity.

children.label: D
children.text: The foundational components of healthy relationships and their impact on the formation of healthy marriages and safe and stable families.

children.label: E
children.text: How other youth risk behaviors, such as drug and alcohol usage, increase the risk for teen sex.

children.label: F
children.text: How to resist and avoid, and receive help regarding, sexual coercion and dating violence, recognizing that even with consent teen sex remains a youth risk behavior.

children.label: A
children.text: any information provided on contraception is medically accurate and complete and ensures that students understand that contraception offers physical risk reduction, but not risk elimination; and

children.label: B
children.text: the education does not include demonstrations, simulations, or distribution of contraceptive devices.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A State or other entity receiving an allotment pursuant to subsection (a) may use up to 20 percent of such allotment to build the evidence base for sexual risk avoidance education by conducting or supporting research.

children.label: i
children.text: rigorous;

children.label: ii
children.text: evidence-based; and

children.label: iii
children.text: designed and conducted by independent researchers who have experience in conducting and publishing research in peer-reviewed outlets.

children.label: A
children.text: collect information on the programs and activities funded through the allotment; and

children.label: B
children.text: submit reports to the Secretary on the data from such programs and activities.

children.label: A
children.text: in consultation with appropriate State and local agencies, conduct one or more rigorous evaluations of the education funded through this section and associated data; and

children.label: B
children.text: submit a report to the Congress on the results of such evaluations, together with a summary of the information collected pursuant to subsection (b)(6).

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: 2 Consultation
children.text: In conducting the evaluations required by paragraph (1), including the establishment of rigorous evaluation methodologies, the Secretary shall consult with relevant stakeholders and evaluation experts.

Sections 703, 707, and 708 of this title apply to allotments under subsection (a) to the same extent and in the same manner as such sections apply to allotments under , except that shall be applied by substituting “the total of the sums” for “four-sevenths of the total of the sums”.section 702(c) of this titlesection 703(a) of this title
Sections 705 and 706 of this title apply to allotments under subsection (a) to the extent determined by the Secretary to be appropriate.

The term “age-appropriate” means suitable (in terms of topics, messages, and teaching methods) to the developmental and social maturity of the particular age or age group of children or adolescents, based on developing cognitive, emotional, and behavioral capacity typical for the age or age group.

children.label: A
children.text: published in peer-reviewed journals, where applicable; or

children.label: B
children.text: comprising information that leading professional organizations and agencies with relevant expertise in the field recognize as accurate, objective, and complete.

children.label: A
children.text: established scientific methods for measuring the impact of an intervention or program model in changing behavior (specifically sexual activity or other sexual risk behaviors), or reducing pregnancy, among youth; or

children.label: B
children.text: other evidence-based methodologies established by the Secretary for purposes of this section.
The term “youth” refers to one or more individuals who have attained age 10 but not age 20.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: To carry out this section, there is appropriated, out of any money in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, $75,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2018 through 2026, and for the period beginning on , and ending on , an amount equal to the pro rata portion of the amount appropriated for the corresponding period for fiscal year 2026.October 1, 2026December 31, 2026

children.heading: Reservation
children.label: 2 Reservation
children.text: The Secretary shall reserve, for each of fiscal years 2018 through 2023, and for the applicable period described in paragraph (1), not more than 20 percent of the amount appropriated pursuant to paragraph (1) for administering the program under this section, including the conducting of national evaluations and the provision of technical assistance to the recipients of allotments.
